subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF Art IV, World Bank, RSF, Freedom House, HRW, Amnesty, TI, Arab Center/MEI); Omani state media EXCLUDED there, with economic figures admitted only via IMF/rating-agency validation.
A genuine fiscal-consolidation success (deficits to surplus, debt cut sharply, an investment-grade upgrade) and a trusted neutral-mediator diplomacy.
Standout features: fiscal repair, regional diplomacy, and very low crime, against an absolute-monarchy rights ceiling, restricted press, and extreme heat/water and cyclone exposure.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), a fragile/violated 8 Apr ceasefire, and the global energy shock. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: A genuine fiscal-repair success and a credible diversification push, with a USD peg the autonomy trade-off.

fiscal_state.notes: Oil-revenue budget squeezed by the export disruption. (IMF)

monetary_independence.notes: A hard USD peg means essentially no independent monetary policy - stable but no tool. (IMF Art IV)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Peg-delivered credibility; the rial is not internationally held. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Sound and well-capitalised per the IMF. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Oil exports and the Sohar/transit economy depend on Hormuz traffic that has nearly stopped. (Reuters; EIA)

institutional.notes: Low corruption and capable administration, against no political-sphere judicial independence and restricted press.

rule_of_law.notes: An absolute sultanate with no judicial independence on political matters, but low corruption and functional commercial law. (TI CPI 2025; Freedom House)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A capable administration with an effective Omanization push. (IMF; World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: RSF ~127th, with a media law granting broad censorship powers. (RSF 2026; Freedom House 2025)

political_social.notes: A stable, well-regarded sultanate, against no political rights and a migrant-majority workforce.

political_stability.notes: A stable, well-regarded sultanate (a smooth 2020 succession); managed stability, with longer-term diversification/jobs strain. (Freedom House)

civil_liberties.notes: No parties and criminalised criticism, with citizenship-revocation for critics. (Freedom House 2025; Amnesty)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Easy entry and no income tax draw a large migrant workforce (~43% of the population), but under kafala-legacy controls with no real citizenship path. (HRW; Amnesty)

social_cohesion.notes: Orderly, cohesive, and low-crime, with a large transient migrant component. (analyst; Freedom House)

demographics.notes: A young-ish citizen base creating labour supply but Omanization pressure. (IMF; World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A trusted neutral mediator with an open account, against Yemen-border/Hormuz exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: A trusted neutral mediator (a US-Iran venue, Yemen talks), Western-friendly and low-drama - a genuine strength. (Arab Center; MEI)

conflict_involvement.notes: Controls the southern shore of the Strait of Hormuz; the traditional mediator is now ringed by the 2026 war, with shipping through its waters largely halted. (Reuters)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Open, unsanctioned, with free capital flows. (IMF Art IV)

physical_practical.notes: Good healthcare and very low crime, against extreme heat, water scarcity, and cyclone exposure.

climate.notes: Extreme heat, intensifying Arabian-Sea cyclones, water scarcity, near-unliveable wet-bulb risk offset by wealth/AC. (IPCC AR6; WB)

healthcare.notes: Well-developed, with good outcomes and coverage. (WHO/World Bank consensus)

infrastructure.notes: Desalination-reliant and exposed to the regional water-infrastructure threat, though less central a target than the inner-Gulf states. (CSIS)

crime_safety.notes: Very low crime - a clear strength. (advisory consensus)

disaster_insurance.notes: High cyclone/flood exposure against low insurance penetration. (Carnegie; IEA)

summary: A stable sultanate that has pulled off a genuine fiscal repair - turning deficits to surplus, cutting debt sharply, and earning an investment-grade upgrade - while pursuing a credible Vision-2040 diversification and punching above its weight as a trusted neutral mediator. The offsets are an absolute-monarchy rights ceiling with no political parties and criminalised criticism, a restricted press, a migrant-majority workforce under kafala-legacy controls with no path to belonging, and an extreme heat, water-scarcity, and Arabian Sea cyclone tail. A USD peg trades monetary autonomy for stability. Very low crime is a clear strength.
